title: ‘It was very painful’: Yazidi woman testifies on IS group sexual slavery in Paris court
description: A Yazidi woman held as a sex slave by the Islamic State (IS) group recounted her harrowing ordeal before a French court Thursday, detailing her abduction, repeated rapes and forced sales. The trial of French jihadist Sabri Essid, prosecuted in absentia for genocide and crimes against humanity, marks France’s first case on IS group atrocities against the Yazidis.

title: Senegal not losing without a fight
description: In tonight's program, a joint statement from the Democratic Republic of Congo and Rwanda, where both nations have agreed to take steps to advance last year's stalled peace accord. Also, Senegal says it's not losing its 2025 Africa Cup of Nations title without a fight. And in the Kenyan capital, a groundbreaking menstrual leave policy for government employees is making a difference and proving a success.

title: Gas prices soar as energy infrastructure is attacked in Gulf
description: Natural gas prices soared, as attacks intensified on energy infrastracture in the Middle East. The Ras Laffan plant in Qatar has sustained 'significant damage,' according to its operators, which could take years to fully repair. That will have a significant impact on global supplies, because the facility produces around a fifth of the world's LNG. Also in the show - the World Trade Organization warns a trade slowdown could lead to mounting food i

title: Analysis: Naim Qassem says Hezbollah will continue fighting Israel, frames war as defense of Lebanon

description:
Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Thursday defended Israel’s and the US’s campaign against Iran, saying that airstrikes have largely destroyed Iran’s capacity to enrich uranium or produce ballistic missiles.

title: Analysis: West Bloomfield terror attack highlights Hezbollah connections within Shiite Lebanese community in US
description:
Last week, Ayman Mohamad Ghazali, a Lebanese-US dual national, rammed his car into the entrance of Temple Israel, a synagogue in West Bloomfield, Indiana. Ghazali had familial connections to Hezbollah, and evidence shows multiple connections to the US-designated terrorist group among Shiite Lebanese expatriates, including open support for Hezbollah by some individuals within these communities.

title: Paris mayoral race: Socialist frontrunner faces fierce attacks from rivals

Her main rival is Socialist former deputy mayor Emmanuel Gregoire, who came out well ahead of her in the first round of voting on Sunday. FRANCE 24's French Politics Editor Marc Perelman tells us more.

title: Middle East war escalates after attack on key Iranian gas field
description: #Israel hit Iranian's part of the South Pars #gas field, the world's largest offshore reservoir #Tehran shares with #Doha, on March 18. In retaliation for the strikes, #Iran ramped up its attacks on Gulf countries' energy infrastructures, sending #oil prices soaring. Israel also reported strikes on its refineries on March 19.

According to Mr. Everts, the current conflict is an unnecessary war defined by cycles of retaliation that risk expanding beyond their original scope, with serious implications for regional stability and global energy markets. Europe is already feeling the economic fallout, but the deeper concern lies in the possibility of further d

title: Celebrating the diversity of 'la langue française'
description: You’ll hear its unmistakable sound in tiny islands of the South Pacific, bustling capitals in West Africa and chilly ports in the northeast of the Americas. The French language is spoken by more than 390 million people all over the world, and that linguistic community is celebrated by the International Organisation of Francophonie. French content is also increasingly popular on social media, with lifestyle, culture and education influencers shari

title: Senegal to appeal CAF Africa Cup of Nations decision after team stripped of title
description: Senegal's football federation said Thursday that it would appeal the Confederation of African Football (CAF) decision to strip the country's team of its Africa Cup of Nations and award the title to Morocco. Senegal beat Morocco in Rabat on January 18, a victory that was short-lived after CAF reversed Senegal's win two months later.

title: Senegal stripped of AFCON title: Reactions in Dakar
description: In a shock, historic decision, the Confederation of African Football (CAF) announced that it is withdrawing the Africa Cup of Nations trophy from Senegal, awarding it instead to Morocco. The decision follows complaints from the host nation Moroccan, after a highly contested final. The news has left many Senegalese in shock, torn between confusion and anger, as FRANCE 24’s team in Dakar reports.

title: 'Idea that Trump can be led around by Israel is foolishness,' ex-envoy to Iran says
description: In an interview with FRANCE 24, Elliott Abrams, a former special envoy of US President Donald Trump to Iran, dismissed as "foolishness" the idea that Israel led Trump into war with Tehran. Abrams, who served during Trump's first term in office, said he backed the ongoing war, saying that "five presidents in a row" had vowed to prevent a nuclear Iran but Trump "is the first person who's really acting on that".

title: Settler violence surges in the West Bank
description: As the Iran-Israel-US-Lebanon war makes headlines around the world, what is happening in the occupied West Bank? Expropriation of Palestinian land, settler attacks and killings have long been the story of the West Bank, but human rights campaigners and experts say that amid the fog of regional war, attacks are on the rise and Israeli settlers are emboldened. We take a closer look in this special edition of Middle East Matters.

title: 'Disaster: Attacks on gas infrastructure, in Iran and Qatar, may set us back several years'

In the Israeli-occupied West Bank, the first Palestinian deaths in the Middle East war have sharpened a sense of helplessness as they find themselves ensnared in the crossfire of yet another conflict.

title: Hegseth on seeking $200 billion for Iran war: "That number could move"
description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth confirmed the Pentagon would ask Congress for more money to wage war in Iran, though he said the reported $200 billion figure "could move."Why it matters: A request on that scale would likely face stiff opposition from many Democrats and some Republicans, given the unpopularity of the war and the fact that it would be in addition to the Pentagon's existing $1 trillion budget.Driving the news: The Washington Post rep
